text.title: Tech-Debt Budget for Solos Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real deployments of Tech-Debt Budget for Solos. Each ties a symptom to a root cause and a concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Engineer spends a whole cycle on debt; new-feature pipeline starves; growth stalls.
antipattern.root-cause: Cap discipline relaxed under refactor enthusiasm.
antipattern.fix: Restore 20% cap; push debt across multiple cycles; track velocity recovery.

antipattern.symptom: code-comments scattered across 200 files; ops postmortems reveal a known bug logged 6 months ago.
antipattern.fix: Migrate code-comments into ledger; score; decide; close code-comments that were never going to ship.

antipattern.symptom: Easy debt paid; high-impact load-bearing debt deferred; the next incident hits the deferred item.
antipattern.root-cause: No impact-interest scoring; priority by mood.
antipattern.fix: Score every item; sort by impact * interest; allocate top-of-list first.

antipattern.symptom: Ledger last signed 4 months ago; entries no longer match the codebase.
antipattern.fix: Re-sign now; calendar-block monthly review; auto-flag past 35 days.
